Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces, rolling each into a ball, then flatten into a disk shape.
Place a tablespoon of the spiced beef mixture onto the center of each disk, then fold the dough in half and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops with egg wash and bake in a preheated oven at 180°C for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.

meals.name: Tostones con Mojo
meals.description: Crispy fried plantains served with a tangy and herby mojo sauce, perfect for a delicious snack. This dish is a staple in many Latin American countries, with each region having its own variation.
meals.sourceInspiration: Latin American cuisine, specifically the traditional tostones from Puerto Rico and the Dominican Republic.
tips: Make sure to not overcrowd the frying pan, as this can cause the tostones to stick together.
For an extra crispy coating, chill the fried plantains in the refrigerator for 30 minutes before serving.
variations: For a spicy kick, add a diced jalapeño pepper to the mojo sauce.
To make the dish more substantial, serve the tostones with a side of black beans or roasted vegetables.

steps.instruction: Heat the olive oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat, then fry the plantain slices until crispy and golden brown.
Remove the tostones from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
In a bowl, combine the minced garlic, coriander, lime juice, and salt, mixing well to create the mojo sauce.
Serve the tostones with the mojo sauce spooned over the top, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro or scallions if desired.

meals.name: Churros con Cajeta
meals.description: Crispy fried dough pastries coated in cinnamon sugar, served with a side of creamy cajeta for dipping. This sweet treat is a classic Mexican dessert, perfect for satisfying your sweet tooth.
meals.sourceInspiration: Mexican cuisine, specifically the traditional churros from the city of Mexico City.
tips: Make sure to not overcrowd the frying pan, as this can cause the churros to stick together.
For an extra crispy coating, chill the fried churros in the refrigerator for 30 minutes before serving.
variations: For a chocolate dipping sauce, melt 1 cup of dark chocolate chips with 1 tablespoon of heavy cream in a double boiler.
To make the dish more substantial, serve the churros with a side of whipped cream or ice cream.

steps.instruction: Combine the flour, granulated sugar, and 1/2 teaspoon of salt in a bowl, then add the whole milk, mixing until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until smooth, then let it rest for 10 minutes.
Heat the vegetable oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat, then pipe the dough through a star tip to create the churros.
Fry the churros until golden brown, then remove from the oil and place on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
In a saucepan, combine the goat's milk, vanilla extract, and 1 tablespoon of granulated sugar, heating over medium heat until the mixture thickens and turns a deep amber color, creating the cajeta.

meals.name: Tres Leches Cake with Cajeta and Pistachio
meals.description: A moist and decadent sponge cake soaked in three types of milk, topped with a rich cajeta sauce and crunchy pistachios, perfect for special occasions.
meals.sourceInspiration: Traditional Mexican dessert, inspired by the country's love for sweet and creamy treats.
tips: Make sure to not overmix the cake batter to ensure a light and fluffy texture.
Use high-quality cajeta sauce for the best flavor.
variations: Replace the pistachios with chopped almonds or pecans for a different flavor and texture.
Add a sprinkle of cinnamon or cocoa powder on top of the cake for extra flavor.

steps.instruction: Preheat the oven to 180°C and grease a 20cm round cake pan.
Prepare the cake batter by whisking together flour, sugar, and eggs.
Pour the batter into the prepared cake pan and bake for 25-30 minutes.
Remove the cake from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes.
Poke holes in the cake with a skewer and pour the three types of milk over the top.
Refrigerate the cake for at least 2 hours to allow it to absorb the milk.
Top the cake with cajeta sauce and chopped pistachios.

meals.name: Churros with Spiced Chocolate Dipping Sauce
meals.description: Crispy and sweet churros served with a rich and spicy chocolate dipping sauce, perfect for a fun and indulgent treat.
meals.sourceInspiration: Traditional Mexican dessert, inspired by the country's love for sweet and fried treats.
tips: Make sure the oil is at the correct temperature to ensure the churros fry evenly and don't absorb too much oil.
Don't overcrowd the frying pan, fry the churros in batches if necessary.
variations: Add a sprinkle of cinnamon or cocoa powder on top of the churros for extra flavor.
Replace the dark chocolate chips with milk chocolate or white chocolate for a different flavor.

steps.instruction: Heat the vegetable oil in a deep frying pan to 180°C.
Prepare the churro dough by whisking together flour, sugar, and eggs.
Pipe the dough through a star tip into the hot oil, cutting into 10-12cm lengths.
Fry the churros for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown and crispy.
Drain the churros on paper towels and serve with spiced chocolate dipping sauce.
Melt the chocolate chips in a double boiler and whisk in cinnamon and cayenne pepper.

meals.name: Coconut Flan with Lime and Toasted Pecans
meals.description: A creamy and rich coconut flan topped with a tangy lime sauce and crunchy toasted pecans, perfect for a refreshing and indulgent dessert.
meals.sourceInspiration: Traditional Mexican dessert, inspired by the country's love for creamy and sweet treats.
tips: Make sure to not overmix the flan mixture to ensure a smooth and creamy texture.
Use high-quality coconut cream for the best flavor.
variations: Replace the pecans with chopped almonds or walnuts for a different flavor and texture.
Add a sprinkle of cinnamon or cocoa powder on top of the flans for extra flavor.

steps.instruction: Preheat the oven to 180°C and prepare a water bath.
Prepare the flan mixture by whisking together sweetened condensed milk, coconut cream, eggs, and sugar.
Pour the mixture into 6 ramekins and place in the water bath.
Bake the flans for 35-40 minutes, until set and lightly golden.
Remove the flans from the oven and let them cool to room temperature.
Top the flans with a tangy lime sauce and toasted pecans.
Refrigerate the flans for at least 2 hours to allow them to chill and set.

meals.name: Saffron Risotto Cakes
meals.description: Crispy fried risotto cakes infused with saffron and parmesan, perfect as a side dish for special occasions. These Italian-inspired cakes are sure to impress your guests.
meals.sourceInspiration: Italian cuisine, specifically the Veneto region
tips: Use high-quality saffron for the best flavor and color.
Don't overmix the risotto mixture to avoid dense cakes.
variations: Add some diced ham or bacon for a non-vegetarian version.
Use gluten-free breadcrumbs for a gluten-free version.

steps.instruction: Prepare the risotto according to package instructions, then let it cool.
Mix the cooled risotto with saffron, parmesan, and egg.
Shape the risotto mixture into patties, coat with breadcrumbs, and fry until crispy.

meals.name: Grilled Asparagus with Lemon-Parmesan Sauce
meals.description: Tender asparagus grilled to perfection and served with a tangy lemon-parmesan sauce, perfect as a side dish for spring and summer. The brightness of the lemon and the richness of the parmesan complement the asparagus beautifully.
meals.sourceInspiration: Italian cuisine, specifically the Tuscany region
tips: Choose thick asparagus spears for easier grilling.
Don't overcook the asparagus to avoid losing its texture.
variations: Add some chopped herbs like parsley or basil to the lemon-parmesan sauce.
Use gluten-free soy sauce for a gluten-free version.

steps.instruction: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
Grill the asparagus for 3-5 minutes per side, or until tender.
Prepare the lemon-parmesan sauce by mixing lemon juice, parmesan, and garlic.

meals.name: Croissants aux Amandes
meals.description: Flaky, buttery croissants filled with a rich almond cream and topped with a delicate glaze, perfect for a French breakfast.
tips: Keep the dough and butter cold to ensure flaky layers.
Don't overfill the croissants with almond cream, as they will expand during baking.
variations: Replace almond cream with a fruit curd, such as raspberry or strawberry, for a different flavor profile.

steps.instruction: Roll out the chilled dough to a thickness of 3mm and cut into triangles.
Spread a small amount of almond cream on each triangle, leaving a 1cm border around the edges.
Roll each triangle into a croissant shape and place on a parchment-lined baking sheet.
Brush the tops with beaten egg and bake in a preheated oven at 200°C for 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown.

meals.name: Tarte aux Fruits et Crème Fraîche
meals.description: A sweet and tangy tart filled with a mixture of fresh berries and topped with a dollop of crème fraîche, perfect for a French breakfast.
tips: Use a variety of berries, such as strawberries, blueberries, and raspberries, for a colorful and flavorful tart.
Don't overmix the sugar and butter mixture, as it can become tough and dense.
variations: Replace crème fraîche with whipped cream or yogurt for a lighter topping.

steps.instruction: Roll out the chilled dough to a thickness of 3mm and place in a tart pan with a removable bottom.
Mix the confectioners' sugar and softened butter to form a crumbly mixture, then press into the bottom of the tart shell.
Arrange the mixed berries on top of the sugar mixture, leaving a 1cm border around the edges.
Bake in a preheated oven at 180°C for 25-30 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the fruit is tender.

meals.name: Pain au Chocolat
meals.description: A rich and buttery pastry filled with a velvety chocolate center, perfect for a French breakfast.
tips: Use high-quality dark chocolate for the best flavor.
Don't overfill the pastry with chocolate, as it can leak out during baking.
variations: Replace dark chocolate with milk chocolate or white chocolate for a different flavor profile.

steps.instruction: Roll out the chilled dough to a thickness of 3mm and cut into rectangles.
Place a small amount of chopped chocolate in the center of each rectangle, leaving a 1cm border around the edges.
Fold the dough over the chocolate, pressing the edges to seal, and place on a parchment-lined baking sheet.
Brush the tops with beaten egg and bake in a preheated oven at 200°C for 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown.

meals.name: New Orleans-Style Po' Boy Sandwich
meals.description: A crispy French baguette filled with succulent fried oysters, crunchy lettuce, and creamy remoulade sauce, perfect for a satisfying lunch.
tips: Make sure to not overcoat the oysters with the flour mixture, as this can make them heavy and greasy.
Use a thermometer to ensure the oil reaches the correct temperature for frying.
variations: For a gluten-free version, substitute the all-purpose flour with gluten-free flour and use a gluten-free baguette.
For a vegetarian version, replace the oysters with fried portobello mushrooms.

steps.instruction: Prepare the remoulade sauce according to the package instructions.
In a shallow dish, mix together the flour and cornstarch.
Dredge the oysters in the flour mixture, shaking off excess.
Fry the oysters in hot oil until golden brown and crispy.
Assemble the po' boy sandwiches by spreading remoulade sauce on the baguette, followed by a layer of lettuce, a few fried oysters, and finally a slice of tomato.

meals.name: California-Style Grilled Chicken and Avocado Wrap
meals.description: A flavorful and healthy wrap filled with grilled chicken, creamy avocado, and crunchy lettuce, perfect for a light and satisfying lunch.
tips: Make sure to not overcook the chicken, as this can make it dry and tough.
Use a ripe avocado for the best flavor and texture.
variations: For a vegan version, replace the chicken with grilled portobello mushrooms and use a vegan tortilla.
For a low-carb version, use a low-carb tortilla or substitute with a lettuce wrap.

steps.instruction: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
Grill the chicken strips until cooked through, about 5-6 minutes per side.
Slice the avocado and lettuce, and chop the tomato.
Assemble the wraps by spreading a layer of avocado on the tortilla, followed by a few slices of grilled chicken, some lettuce, and finally a slice of tomato.
Drizzle with olive oil and serve immediately.

meals.name: Southern-Style Fried Green Tomato BLT
meals.description: A twist on the classic BLT sandwich, featuring crispy fried green tomatoes, smoky bacon, and creamy mayonnaise, perfect for a satisfying and flavorful lunch.
tips: Make sure to not overcoat the green tomato slices with the flour mixture, as this can make them heavy and greasy.
Use a thermometer to ensure the oil reaches the correct temperature for frying.
variations: For a vegetarian version, replace the bacon with grilled portobello mushrooms.
For a gluten-free version, substitute the all-purpose flour with gluten-free flour and use gluten-free bread.

steps.instruction: Prepare the breading station by mixing together the flour and cornstarch in a shallow dish.
Dredge the green tomato slices in the flour mixture, shaking off excess.
Fry the green tomato slices in hot oil until golden brown and crispy, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Cook the bacon slices in a pan until crispy, about 4-5 minutes per side.
Assemble the BLT sandwiches by spreading a layer of mayonnaise on the bread, followed by a few slices of fried green tomato, a few slices of bacon, and finally a lettuce leaf.

meals.name: Sosatie with Chakalaka Sauce
meals.description: Tender and flavorful lamb skewers served with a spicy and tangy chakalaka sauce, perfect for a South African-inspired dinner.
tips: Make sure to soak the skewers in water for at least 30 minutes before grilling to prevent them from burning.
Use a meat thermometer to ensure the lamb is cooked to a safe internal temperature.
variations: For a vegetarian version, substitute the lamb with portobello mushrooms or eggplant.
For a spicy kick, add a diced jalapeno pepper to the chakalaka sauce.

steps.instruction: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
Thread the lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Brush the lamb with olive oil and season with salt and black pepper.
Grill the lamb skewers for 10-12 minutes per side, or until cooked to desired doneness.
Serve the lamb skewers with chakalaka sauce spooned over the top.

meals.name: Injera with Misir Wot
meals.description: Tender and slightly sour injera bread served with a rich and spicy misir wot stew, perfect for an Ethiopian-inspired dinner.
tips: Make sure to use a high-quality teff flour to get the best flavor and texture from the injera.
Use a thermometer to ensure the oil is at the right temperature for frying the onions.
variations: For a vegan version, substitute the honey with maple syrup.
For a gluten-containing version, substitute the teff flour with all-purpose flour.

steps.instruction: In a large bowl, whisk together the teff flour and water to make the injera batter.
Let the batter rest for 24-48 hours to allow it to ferment.
Heat a non-stick skillet or mitad over medium heat.
Pour a small amount of the injera batter onto the skillet and cook for 1-2 minutes, until the edges start to curl and the surface is dry.
Serve the injera with misir wot stew spooned over the top.

meals.name: Jollof Rice with Chicken and Vegetables
meals.description: Flavorful and spicy jollof rice dish cooked with chicken and vegetables, perfect for a West African-inspired dinner.
tips: Use a high-quality jollof spice blend to get the best flavor from the dish.
Make sure to not overcook the rice, as it can become mushy and unappetizing.
variations: For a vegetarian version, substitute the chicken with tofu or tempeh.
For a spicy kick, add a diced jalapeno pepper to the saucepan.

steps.instruction: Heat the oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the onion, tomato, and bell pepper to the saucepan and cook until the vegetables are tender.
Add the chicken to the saucepan and cook until browned on all sides.
Add the rice, jollof spice blend, salt, and black pepper to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Add 500ml of water to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the saucepan, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.

meals.name: Jerk Chicken Patties
meals.description: Spicy jerk seasoned chicken patties filled with the bold flavors of the Caribbean, perfect for a snack or light meal. These patties are made with tender chicken, aromatic spices, and a hint of scotch bonnet peppers.
tips: Make sure to handle the scotch bonnet peppers carefully, as the oils in the peppers can cause skin irritation.
If you can't find scotch bonnet peppers, you can substitute with a similar type of hot pepper.
variations: For a vegetarian version, substitute the chicken with a plant-based protein source such as tofu or tempeh.
For a gluten-free version, use gluten-free breadcrumbs and flour.

steps.instruction: In a large bowl, combine the minced chicken, scotch bonnet peppers, jerk seasoning, and a pinch of salt. Mix well until just combined.
Divide the mixture into 4 equal parts and shape each part into a patty.
Dip each patty into the flour, then the egg, and finally the breadcrumbs to coat.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Fry the patties until golden brown and cooked through.

meals.name: Makowiec
meals.description: A traditional Polish poppy seed cake filled with a rich, velvety poppy seed filling and topped with a crumbly streusel topping.
tips: Use high-quality poppy seeds for the best flavor and texture.
Don't overmix the streusel topping or it will become tough.
variations: Replace the poppy seeds with chopped nuts for a different flavor and texture.
Add a layer of fruit preserves or jam for added sweetness and flavor.

steps.instruction: Preheat the oven to 180°C and grease a 20cm round cake pan.
Prepare the poppy seed filling by grinding the poppy seeds in a spice grinder and mixing with sugar, eggs, and vanilla extract.
Prepare the streusel topping by mixing flour, sugar, and cold butter until crumbly.
Assemble the cake by spreading the poppy seed filling over the dough, topping with streusel, and baking for 50 minutes.

meals.name: Pryanik
meals.description: A traditional Russian spiced gingerbread cookie filled with a sweet, sticky honey filling and topped with a crumbly powdered sugar glaze.
tips: Use high-quality honey for the best flavor.
Don't overbake the cookies or they will become dry.
variations: Replace the honey with maple syrup for a different flavor.
Add a sprinkle of sea salt for added texture and flavor.

steps.instruction: Preheat the oven to 180°C and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Prepare the dough by mixing flour, baking soda, ginger, and cinnamon, then adding honey and butter until a dough forms.
Roll out the dough to 1cm thickness and cut into desired shapes.
Bake the cookies for 20 minutes, then fill with honey filling and top with powdered sugar glaze.

meals.name: Kourabiedes
meals.description: A traditional Ukrainian shortbread-like cookie filled with a sweet, crumbly walnut filling and topped with a light dusting of powdered sugar.
tips: Use high-quality walnuts for the best flavor and texture.
Don't overbake the cookies or they will become dry.
variations: Replace the walnuts with pecans or hazelnuts for a different flavor.
Add a sprinkle of sea salt for added texture and flavor.

steps.instruction: Preheat the oven to 180°C and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Prepare the dough by mixing flour, butter, and confectioners' sugar until a crumbly mixture forms.
Prepare the walnut filling by grinding walnuts in a spice grinder and mixing with vanilla extract.
Assemble the cookies by filling the dough with walnut filling and baking for 25 minutes.

meals.name: Kålrotsgrottor
meals.description: A traditional Scandinavian side dish featuring caramelized rutabaga and carrots, topped with a crunchy breadcrumb mixture and baked to perfection.
tips: To ensure the breadcrumbs are evenly distributed, sprinkle them over the vegetable mixture in a circular motion.
If using gluten-free breadcrumbs, be sure to check the ingredient label for certification.
variations: For a vegan version, replace the butter with a dairy-free alternative, such as coconut oil or vegan butter.
To add some crunch, sprinkle some chopped nuts or seeds over the breadcrumbs before baking.

steps.instruction: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
In a large bowl, toss the rutabaga and carrots with melted butter, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Transfer the vegetable mixture to a baking dish and top with breadcrumbs.
Bake the Kålrotsgrottor in the preheated oven for 45 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the vegetables are tender.

meals.name: Fennel and Apple Slaw
meals.description: A refreshing Scandinavian side dish featuring thinly sliced fennel and apples, tossed in a zesty dressing and topped with toasted walnuts.
tips: To toast the walnuts, simply spread them on a baking sheet and bake in a preheated oven at 180°C for 5-7 minutes, or until fragrant and lightly browned.
For an extra burst of flavor, add a sprinkle of chopped fresh dill over the top of the slaw before serving.
variations: For a vegan version, replace the honey with a dairy-free alternative, such as maple syrup or coconut sugar.
To add some heat, sprinkle a pinch of red pepper flakes over the top of the slaw before serving.

steps.instruction: In a large bowl, whisk together the apple cider vinegar, honey, salt, and black pepper until well combined.
Add the thinly sliced fennel and apples to the bowl and toss to coat with the dressing.
Sprinkle the toasted walnuts over the top of the slaw and serve immediately.

meals.name: Rødkål
meals.description: A traditional Scandinavian side dish featuring braised red cabbage, apples, and onions, flavored with spices and vinegar.
tips: To enhance the flavor, add a pinch of ground cinnamon or nutmeg to the vinegar mixture before pouring it over the cabbage.
For a sweeter Rødkål, add an extra 10-20g of sugar to the vinegar mixture.
variations: For a vegan version, replace the sugar with a dairy-free alternative, such as maple syrup or coconut sugar.
To add some heat, sprinkle a pinch of red pepper flakes over the top of the Rødkål before serving.

steps.instruction: In a large saucepan, combine the shredded red cabbage, diced apples, and diced onions.
In a small bowl, whisk together the apple cider vinegar, sugar, salt, and black pepper until well combined.
Pour the vinegar mixture over the cabbage mixture and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20-25 minutes, or until the cabbage is tender and the liquid has been absorbed.

meals.name: Full English Breakfast with Black Pudding
meals.description: A hearty, traditional British breakfast featuring black pudding, bacon, eggs, and sausages, perfect for a weekend morning. This dish is a staple of British cuisine and is sure to satisfy any appetite.
tips: Use high-quality ingredients, such as thick-cut bacon and fresh sausages, to ensure the best flavor.
Cook the ingredients in batches to prevent overcrowding the pans.
Consider using a thermometer to ensure the sausages are cooked to a safe internal temperature.
variations: For a vegetarian version, replace the black pudding and sausages with grilled portobello mushrooms and roasted vegetables.
For a gluten-free version, replace the toasted bread with gluten-free bread or crackers.

steps.instruction: Cook the sausages in a large pan over medium heat, browning on all sides.
Add the sliced black pudding to the pan and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Cook the bacon in a separate pan until crispy, then set aside.
Fry the eggs in the same pan as the bacon, sunny-side up.
Grill the tomatoes and mushrooms until tender.
Toast the bread and spread with butter.
Heat the baked beans in a saucepan over low heat.
Assemble the full English breakfast by placing the cooked ingredients on a large plate or platter.

meals.name: Scottish Smoked Salmon with Scrambled Eggs and Toast
meals.description: A delicious and elegant breakfast dish featuring smoked salmon, scrambled eggs, and toasted bread, perfect for a special occasion. This dish is a staple of Scottish cuisine and is sure to impress any guest.
tips: Use high-quality smoked salmon for the best flavor.
Don't overcook the eggs, as they can become dry and rubbery.
Consider adding a sprinkle of capers or chopped red onion for added flavor and texture.
variations: For a gluten-free version, replace the toasted bread with gluten-free bread or crackers.
For a dairy-free version, replace the cream with a non-dairy alternative such as almond milk or soy milk.

steps.instruction: Scramble the eggs in a bowl and set aside.
Melt the butter in a pan over medium heat and add the scrambled eggs.
Cook the eggs until they are just set, then remove from heat.
Toast the bread until lightly browned.
Assemble the dish by placing the scrambled eggs on top of the toasted bread, followed by a slice of smoked salmon.
Drizzle with lemon juice and sprinkle with chopped fresh dill.

meals.name: Welsh Rarebit with Poached Eggs and Toast
meals.description: A delicious and comforting breakfast dish featuring a rich and creamy Welsh rarebit sauce, poached eggs, and toasted bread, perfect for a cold winter morning. This dish is a staple of Welsh cuisine and is sure to warm any heart.
tips: Use a high-quality cheddar cheese for the best flavor.
Don't overcook the sauce, as it can become too thick and sticky.
Consider adding a sprinkle of paprika or chopped fresh herbs for added flavor and color.
variations: For a gluten-free version, replace the flour with a gluten-free alternative such as cornstarch or tapioca flour.
For a dairy-free version, replace the cheese and milk with non-dairy alternatives such as soy cheese and almond milk.

steps.instruction: Melt the butter in a saucepan over medium heat.
Add the flour and whisk to combine, cooking for 1-2 minutes.
Slowly add the milk, whisking constantly to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until it thickens, stirring occasionally.
Remove the sauce from the heat and stir in the grated cheese until melted and smooth.
Poach the eggs in a separate pot of simmering water.
Assemble the dish by placing the toasted bread on a plate, topping with a poached egg, and spooning the Welsh rarebit sauce over the top.

meals.name: Bulgogi Beef with Gochujang Sauce
meals.description: Tender beef short ribs marinated in a sweet and spicy gochujang sauce, served with steamed rice and kimchi. This dish is a classic Korean favorite that is sure to please.
tips: Make sure to marinate the beef for at least 2 hours to allow the flavors to penetrate the meat.
Use a meat thermometer to ensure the beef is cooked to a safe internal temperature.
Serve with additional kimchi and gochujang sauce on the side for added flavor.
variations: For a vegetarian version, substitute the beef with portobello mushrooms or eggplant.
For a spicy kick, add more gochujang sauce to the marinade.

steps.instruction: In a large bowl, whisk together gochujang sauce, soy sauce, brown sugar, garlic, and ginger to make the marinade.
Add the beef short ribs to the marinade and mix well to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat. Remove the beef from the marinade and cook for 5-7 minutes per side, or until cooked to desired level of doneness.
While the beef is cooking, cook the rice according to package instructions. Serve the beef with steamed rice and kimchi.

meals.name: Jeyuk Bokkeum (Stir-Fried Pork with Kimchi)
meals.description: A spicy and savory stir-fry made with marinated pork, kimchi, and vegetables, served with steamed rice. This dish is a popular Korean comfort food that is easy to make and delicious to eat.
tips: Use a high-quality kimchi that is spicy and sour for the best flavor.
Don't overcook the pork, as it can become tough and dry.
Add other vegetables such as carrots or zucchini to the stir-fry for added flavor and nutrition.
variations: For a vegetarian version, substitute the pork with tofu or tempeh.
For a spicy kick, add more gochujang sauce to the marinade.

steps.instruction: In a large bowl, whisk together gochujang sauce, soy sauce, brown sugar, garlic, and ginger to make the marinade.
Add the pork to the marinade and mix well to coat. Let it marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Heat the sesame oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add the marinated pork and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the kimchi to the skillet and stir-fry for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the kimchi is slightly caramelized.
Serve the pork and kimchi over steamed rice.

meals.name: Mandu (Korean Dumplings) with Dipping Sauce
meals.description: Pan-fried Korean dumplings filled with a savory mixture of pork, kimchi, and vegetables, served with a sweet and spicy dipping sauce. This dish is a popular Korean street food that is easy to make and delicious to eat.
tips: Use a high-quality kimchi that is spicy and sour for the best flavor.
Don't overfill the dumplings, as they can burst open during cooking.
Add other ingredients such as carrots or zucchini to the pork mixture for added flavor and nutrition.
variations: For a vegetarian version, substitute the pork with tofu or tempeh.
For a spicy kick, add more gochujang sauce to the dipping sauce.

steps.instruction: In a large bowl, combine the chopped pork, kimchi, gochujang sauce, soy sauce, brown sugar, garlic, and ginger. Mix well to combine.
Lay a dumpling wrapper on a flat surface and place a tablespoon of the pork mixture in the center of the wrapper.

Dip your finger in a small amount of water and run it along the edge of the wrapper. Fold the wrapper into a triangle by bringing the two opposite corners together to form a point. Press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Heat the sesame oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. When the oil is hot, add the dumplings and cook until the bottoms are golden brown, about 2-3 minutes.
Serve the dumplings with the dipping sauce.

meals.name: Grilled Wagyu Beef with Miso Glaze
meals.description: Savory grilled Wagyu beef glazed with a sweet and savory miso sauce, served with steamed bok choy and Japanese rice.
tips: Make sure to handle the Wagyu beef gently to avoid damaging the fat marbling.
Adjust the amount of miso paste to your taste, as it can be quite salty.
variations: For a vegetarian version, substitute the Wagyu beef with portobello mushrooms or eggplant.
For a gluten-free version, use gluten-free soy sauce instead of sake.

steps.instruction: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
In a small saucepan, whisk together miso paste, sake, mirin, and brown sugar. Bring to a boil over medium heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
Grill the Wagyu beef for 4-5 minutes per side, or until cooked to desired doneness.
Brush the miso glaze over the beef during the last minute of grilling.
Steam the bok choy until tender, then serve with the grilled beef and Japanese rice.

meals.name: Pan-Seared Sushi-Grade Tuna with Yuzu Sauce
meals.description: Seared sushi-grade tuna served with a tangy and citrusy yuzu sauce, steamed asparagus, and Japanese rice.
tips: Make sure to handle the sushi-grade tuna gently to avoid damaging the flesh.
Adjust the amount of yuzu juice to your taste, as it can be quite tart.
variations: For a vegetarian version, substitute the tuna with portobello mushrooms or eggplant.
For a gluten-free version, use gluten-free soy sauce instead of regular soy sauce.

steps.instruction: Heat a skillet over high heat.
Add a small amount of oil to the skillet, then sear the tuna for 2-3 minutes per side, or until cooked to desired doneness.
In a small bowl, whisk together yuzu juice, soy sauce, and rice vinegar.
Steam the asparagus until tender, then serve with the seared tuna and Japanese rice.
Drizzle the yuzu sauce over the tuna and serve immediately.

meals.name: Braised Pork Belly with Pickled Vegetables
meals.description: Tender braised pork belly served with a variety of pickled vegetables, steamed bok choy, and Japanese rice.
tips: Make sure to handle the pork belly gently to avoid damaging the fat.
Adjust the amount of pickling vinegar to your taste, as it can be quite sour.
variations: For a vegetarian version, substitute the pork belly with portobello mushrooms or eggplant.
For a gluten-free version, use gluten-free soy sauce instead of regular soy sauce.

steps.instruction: Preheat the oven to 300°F (150°C).
In a large Dutch oven, combine pork belly, soy sauce, sake, mirin, and brown sugar. Cover and braise for 2 hours, or until tender.
In a small bowl, whisk together pickling vinegar, salt, and sugar. Add sliced cucumber, carrots, and daikon radish, and let pickle for at least 30 minutes.
Steam the bok choy until tender, then serve with the braised pork belly and pickled vegetables.
Serve the pork belly with steamed Japanese rice and pickled vegetables.

meals.name: Szechuan Spicy Wontons
meals.description: Delicate wontons filled with a savory mixture of pork and spices, served in a numbing Szechuan chili oil broth.
meals.sourceInspiration: Szechuan cuisine, known for its bold flavors and spicy dishes.
tips: Use high-quality wonton wrappers for the best texture.
Adjust the level of spiciness to your liking.
variations: Add some diced vegetables, such as cabbage or carrots, to the filling for extra flavor and nutrition.

steps.instruction: Prepare the filling by mixing ground pork, toasted Szechuan peppercorns, soy sauce, and salt.
Assemble the wontons by placing a small spoonful of filling in the center of each wrapper.
Cook the wontons in boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then serve in Szechuan chili oil broth.

meals.name: Cantonese-Style Roast Pork Puffs
meals.description: Flaky, buttery pastry puffs filled with a sweet and savory roast pork filling, perfect as a snack or appetizer.
meals.sourceInspiration: Cantonese cuisine, known for its delicate flavors and intricate pastry work.
tips: Use high-quality puff pastry for the best texture.
Don't overfill the puffs, as they will expand during baking.
variations: Add some diced vegetables, such as carrots or peas, to the filling for extra flavor and nutrition.

steps.instruction: Prepare the filling by mixing roast pork, hoisin sauce, sesame oil, and chopped scallions.
Roll out the puff pastry and cut into squares.
Assemble the puffs by placing a small spoonful of filling in the center of each square, then brushing the edges with egg wash and folding the pastry in half.
Bake the puffs in a preheated oven at 400°F (200°C) for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.

meals.name: Xinjiang-Style Lamb Kebabs
meals.description: Tender and flavorful lamb kebabs, marinated in a mixture of cumin, coriander, and chili peppers, grilled to perfection.
meals.sourceInspiration: Xinjiang cuisine, known for its bold flavors and grilled meats.
tips: Use high-quality lamb for the best flavor.
Don't overcrowd the skewers, as this can affect the cooking time.
variations: Add some diced onions or bell peppers to the kebabs for extra flavor and nutrition.

steps.instruction: Prepare the marinade by mixing cumin seeds, coriander seeds, chili peppers, garlic, and yogurt.
Marinate the lamb cubes in the mixture for at least 30 minutes.
Thread the marinated lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Grill the kebabs over medium-high heat for 5-7 minutes per side, or until cooked through.

meals.name: Khao Niew Mamuang
meals.description: A classic Thai dessert featuring sweet and fragrant mango served with creamy coconut sticky rice, perfect for warm weather.
meals.sourceInspiration: Thai cuisine, specifically the street food scene in Bangkok
tips: Use short-grain glutinous rice for the best results.
Adjust the amount of palm sugar to your taste.
variations: Add a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds for extra crunch.
Replace mango with other sweet and fragrant fruits like durian or jackfruit.

steps.instruction: Rinse the glutinous rice thoroughly and soak it in water for at least 4 hours.
Steam the glutinous rice until it's cooked and fluffy.
In a saucepan, combine coconut milk, palm sugar, and salt. Heat the mixture over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the sugar dissolves.
Add the cooked glutinous rice to the coconut milk mixture and stir well.
Serve the coconut sticky rice with sliced mango.

meals.name: Tub Tim Krob
meals.description: A refreshing and colorful Thai dessert featuring water chestnuts, coconut milk, and crushed ice, perfect for hot summer days.
meals.sourceInspiration: Thai cuisine, specifically the desserts served at traditional Thai festivals
tips: Use fresh water chestnuts for the best flavor and texture.
Adjust the amount of palm sugar to your taste.
variations: Add a splash of coconut cream for extra richness.
Replace water chestnuts with other fruits like palm seeds or lotus seeds.

steps.instruction: Combine water chestnuts, coconut milk, palm sugar, and red food coloring in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the sugar dissolves.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 5 minutes.
Let the mixture cool to room temperature.
Stir in crushed ice and serve the dessert chilled.
Garnish with diced jackfruit, if using.

meals.name: Braised Lotus Stem with Fermented Soybeans
meals.description: Tender lotus stem braised in a rich and savory fermented soybean sauce, perfect as a side dish for special occasions. The combination of textures and flavors will leave you wanting more.
meals.sourceInspiration: This recipe is inspired by the traditional Vietnamese dish 'Đậu hũ non' and incorporates the use of fermented soybeans, a common ingredient in Vietnamese cuisine.
tips: Use a high-quality fermented soybean product for the best flavor.
Adjust the amount of fish sauce to your taste, as it can be quite salty.
variations: For a vegan version, replace the fish sauce with a vegan alternative such as tamari or nama shoyu.
Add some heat to the dish by incorporating sliced chili peppers or red pepper flakes.

steps.instruction: Heat the oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add the garlic and ginger and sauté until fragrant.
Add the fermented soybeans and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add the lotus stem, fish sauce, and lime juice. Pour in enough water to cover the ingredients and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 30-40 minutes or until the lotus stem is tender.

meals.name: Grilled Eggplant with Chili Sauce and Herbs
meals.description: Smoky grilled eggplant smothered in a spicy and aromatic chili sauce, topped with fresh herbs and perfect as a side dish for any occasion. The combination of flavors and textures will leave you wanting more.
meals.sourceInspiration: This recipe is inspired by the traditional Vietnamese dish 'Cà tím nướng' and incorporates the use of grilled eggplant and chili sauce, common ingredients in Vietnamese cuisine.
tips: Use a variety of eggplant that holds its shape well when grilled, such as Japanese or Italian eggplant.
Adjust the level of spiciness in the chili sauce to your taste by adding more or less chili flakes.
variations: For a gluten-free version, replace the fish sauce with a gluten-free alternative such as tamari or coconut aminos.
Add some crunch to the dish by topping the eggplant with toasted peanuts or cashews.

steps.instruction: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat. Brush the eggplant slices with oil and season with salt.
Grill the eggplant for 3-4 minutes per side, or until tender and lightly charred.
In a small bowl, whisk together the chili sauce, fish sauce, and lime juice.
Brush the grilled eggplant with the chili sauce mixture and top with garlic, Thai basil, and chili flakes.

meals.name: Stir-Fried Water Spinach with Garlic and Chili
meals.description: Quick and flavorful stir-fry of water spinach, garlic, and chili, perfect as a side dish for any occasion. The combination of textures and flavors will leave you wanting more.
meals.sourceInspiration: This recipe is inspired by the traditional Vietnamese dish 'Rau muống xào tỏi' and incorporates the use of stir-fried water spinach and garlic, common ingredients in Vietnamese cuisine.
tips: Use a high-quality vegetable oil with a high smoke point, such as peanut or avocado oil, to prevent burning.
Adjust the level of spiciness in the dish by adding more or less chili peppers.
variations: For a gluten-free version, replace the soy sauce with a gluten-free alternative such as tamari or coconut aminos.
Add some crunch to the dish by topping the water spinach with toasted peanuts or cashews.

steps.instruction: Heat the oil in a large skillet or wok over high heat. Add the garlic and chili peppers and stir-fry until fragrant.
Add the water spinach and stir-fry until wilted, about 2-3 minutes.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.

meals.name: Stuffed Paratha with Spiced Potatoes and Peas
meals.description: Flaky, buttery paratha stuffed with a flavorful mixture of spiced potatoes, peas, and onions, perfect for a hearty Indian breakfast.
tips: Use a high-quality whole wheat flour to get the best flavor and texture.
Don't overfill the paratha, as it can be difficult to seal and cook evenly.
variations: Add some grated carrot or beetroot to the filling for extra flavor and nutrition.
Use different types of flour, such as all-purpose flour or millet flour, to create a unique flavor and texture.

steps.instruction: Combine flour, ghee or oil, and salt in a large mixing bowl. Gradually add water to form a smooth dough.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes soft and pliable.
Divide the dough into 4 equal portions and roll out each portion into a thin circle.
Heat a non-stick pan or tava over medium heat. Cook the paratha for 1-2 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
Prepare the filling by heating oil in a pan, adding cumin seeds, ginger, and onion. Cook until the onion is translucent.
Add mashed potatoes, peas, coriander powder, garam masala, and salt to the pan. Cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Place a portion of the filling in the center of each paratha circle. Fold the dough over the filling to form a triangle or square shape.
Cook the stuffed paratha in a non-stick pan or tava over medium heat, until golden brown on both sides.

meals.name: Idli with Coconut Chutney and Sambar
meals.description: Steamed idli served with a creamy coconut chutney and a flavorful sambar, a classic South Indian breakfast combination.
tips: Use a high-quality idli rice and urad dal to get the best flavor and texture.
Let the batter ferment for at least 8 hours to get the right consistency and flavor.
variations: Add some chopped onions or carrots to the idli batter for extra flavor and nutrition.
Use different types of chutney, such as tomato or peanut chutney, to create a unique flavor combination.

ingredients.name: vegetables (such as carrots, potatoes, and onions)

steps.instruction: Grind the idli rice and urad dal into a smooth batter using a wet grinder or blender.
Add fenugreek seeds, salt, and water to the batter. Mix well and let it ferment overnight.
Prepare the coconut chutney by grinding coconut, green chilies, ginger, and curry leaves into a smooth paste.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds, turmeric, and sambar powder. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cooked toor dal, vegetables, tamarind paste, and water to the pan. Cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Steam the idli batter in a steamer or idli maker for 15-20 minutes, until cooked through.
Serve the idli with coconut chutney and sambar.

meals.name: Poha with Nuts and Dried Fruits
meals.description: A flavorful and nutritious breakfast dish made with flattened rice, nuts, and dried fruits, perfect for a quick and easy Indian breakfast.
tips: Use a high-quality poha to get the best flavor and texture.
Add some chopped fresh herbs, such as cilantro or mint, to the poha for extra flavor and nutrition.
variations: Add some grated coconut or chopped fresh chilies to the poha for extra flavor and texture.
Use different types of nuts or dried fruits to create a unique flavor combination.

steps.instruction: Rinse the poha in water and drain well.
Heat oil in a pan and add mustard seeds, turmeric, and curry leaves. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chopped onion, ginger, and green chilies to the pan. Cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the poha, nuts, and dried fruits to the pan. Cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add salt, water, and lemon juice to the pan. Cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Serve the poha hot, garnished with chopped nuts and dried fruits.

meals.name: Doner Kebab
meals.description: A classic Turkish dish made with layers of lamb or beef stacked and cooked on a vertical spit, served in a crispy sesame-topped bun with fresh vegetables and tangy sauce.
meals.sourceInspiration: Turkish cuisine, specifically the city of Bursa where doner kebab originated
tips: Make sure to cook the doner kebab meat at a consistent temperature to achieve the perfect crispy exterior and juicy interior.
Use a high-quality pita bread that is crispy on the outside and soft on the inside.
variations: For a vegetarian version, substitute the lamb or beef with sliced eggplant or zucchini.
For a gluten-free version, use gluten-free pita bread or substitute with a gluten-free wrap.

steps.instruction: Prepare the doner kebab meat by layering the sliced lamb or beef on a vertical spit.
Cook the doner kebab meat for 2 hours, or until it is cooked through and crispy on the outside.
Prepare the vegetables by slicing the onion, tomato, and cucumber.
Assemble the doner kebab by slicing the cooked meat and serving it in a pita bread with the prepared vegetables and a dollop of tangy sauce.

meals.name: Manti
meals.description: Turkish dumplings filled with spiced meat and onions, served with yogurt and garlic sauce.
meals.sourceInspiration: Turkish cuisine, specifically the Central Asian tradition of dumpling-making
tips: Make sure to knead the dough long enough to develop the gluten, which will help the dumplings hold their shape.
Use a high-quality yogurt that is thick and creamy, which will help to balance the flavors of the dish.
variations: For a vegetarian version, substitute the ground meat with sautéed mushrooms or spinach.
For a gluten-free version, use gluten-free flour or substitute with a gluten-free wrap.

steps.instruction: Prepare the dumpling dough by mixing the flour, egg, and water.
Prepare the filling by mixing the ground meat, onion, garlic, salt, black pepper, and paprika.
Assemble the dumplings by wrapping the filling in the dough.
Cook the dumplings in boiling water for 10-15 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
Serve the dumplings with yogurt and garlic sauce.

meals.name: Moussaka
meals.description: A rich and satisfying Greek classic, layered with tender eggplant, flavorful ground lamb, and a creamy bechamel sauce.
tips: To prevent the eggplant from becoming too bitter, sprinkle it with salt and let it sit for 30 minutes before rinsing and cooking.
Use high-quality ingredients, such as fresh eggplant and real parmesan cheese, to ensure the best flavor and texture.
variations: For a vegetarian version, substitute the ground lamb with sliced portobello mushrooms or eggplant.
To make the dish more substantial, serve with a side of roasted potatoes or a green salad.

steps.instruction: Preheat the oven to 180°C.
Prepare the eggplant slices by brushing them with olive oil and seasoning with salt and pepper.
Cook the eggplant slices in a large skillet over medium heat until they are golden brown and tender.
Prepare the ground lamb by cooking it in a large skillet over medium-high heat, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the chopped onion and minced garlic to the skillet with the ground lamb and cook until the onion is translucent.
Stir in the tomato puree and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Assemble the moussaka by creating a layer of eggplant slices in the bottom of a large baking dish, followed by a layer of the ground lamb mixture, and finally a layer of bechamel sauce.
Repeat the layers two more times, finishing with a layer of bechamel sauce on top.
Sprinkle the parmesan cheese over the top of the bechamel sauce and bake the moussaka in the preheated oven for 45 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the sauce is bubbly.

meals.name: Tortilla de Patatas con Chorizo
meals.description: A rich and savory Spanish omelette filled with chorizo, potatoes, and onions, perfect for a satisfying snack. This advanced recipe requires some skill and patience, but the result is well worth the effort.
meals.sourceInspiration: This recipe is inspired by the traditional Spanish cuisine, particularly the tapas dishes from the northern regions of Spain.
tips: Use high-quality ingredients, such as Spanish chorizo and fresh eggs, to get the best flavor and texture.
Don't overcook the tortilla, as it can become dry and rubbery.
Let the tortilla rest for a few minutes before slicing and serving, to allow the flavors to meld together.
variations: Add some diced bell peppers or mushrooms to the pan with the onions and potatoes for added flavor and texture.
Use different types of cheese, such as Manchego or Idiazabal, to add a rich and creamy element to the tortilla.

steps.instruction: Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat and cook the diced onions until they are softened and translucent.
Add the diced potatoes to the pan and continue to cook for another 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until they are tender and lightly browned.
Add the sliced chorizo to the pan and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it is lightly browned and crispy.
In a large bowl, beat the eggs and season with salt and black pepper. Add the cooked potato and chorizo mixture to the bowl and stir to combine.
Pour the egg mixture into the frying pan and cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the tortilla is almost set.

meals.name: Croquetas de Pollo
meals.description: Crispy and creamy chicken croquettes, filled with shredded chicken, onions, and spices, perfect for a satisfying snack. This advanced recipe requires some skill and patience, but the result is well worth the effort.
meals.sourceInspiration: This recipe is inspired by the traditional Spanish cuisine, particularly the tapas dishes from the northern regions of Spain.
tips: Use high-quality ingredients, such as fresh chicken and real butter, to get the best flavor and texture.
Don't overmix the dough, as it can become tough and dense.
If the croquettes are not crispy enough, you can try frying them again for a few minutes or until they are golden brown.
variations: Add some diced ham or bacon to the dough for added flavor and texture.
Use different types of cheese, such as Manchego or Idiazabal, to add a rich and creamy element to the croquettes.

steps.instruction: In a large bowl, combine the shredded chicken, diced onions, minced garlic, paprika, salt, and black pepper. Mix well to combine.
Add the all-purpose flour to the bowl and mix until the mixture is well coated.
Add the butter to the bowl and mix until the mixture is well combined and forms a dough.
Beat the eggs in a separate bowl and add them to the dough. Mix until the dough is smooth and well combined.
Divide the dough into small portions and shape each portion into a ball. Then, flatten each ball into a disk shape.
Dip each disk into the breadcrumbs, coating both sides evenly.
Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat and fry the croquettes until they are golden brown and crispy.

meals.name: Patatas Bravas
meals.description: Spicy fried potatoes in a rich and creamy tomato sauce, perfect for a satisfying snack. This advanced recipe requires some skill and patience, but the result is well worth the effort.
meals.sourceInspiration: This recipe is inspired by the traditional Spanish cuisine, particularly the tapas dishes from the northern regions of Spain.
tips: Use high-quality ingredients, such as fresh potatoes and real tomato sauce, to get the best flavor and texture.
Don't overcook the potatoes, as they can become dry and mushy.
If the sauce is too thick, you can try adding a little bit of water or broth to thin it out.
variations: Add some diced chorizo or morcilla to the pan for added flavor and texture.
Use different types of cheese, such as Manchego or Idiazabal, to add a rich and creamy element to the patatas bravas.

steps.instruction: Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over medium heat and cook the diced onions until they are softened and translucent.
Add the diced potatoes to the pan and continue to cook for another 10-12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until they are tender and lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic, smoked paprika, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper to the pan and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the garlic is fragrant.
Add the tomato sauce to the pan and stir to combine. Bring the sauce to a simmer and cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce has thickened and the potatoes are fully coated.
Serve the patatas bravas hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or chives, if desired.

meals.name: Saffron Crème Brûlée Tarts with Matcha and Black Sesame
meals.description: A rich and creamy saffron-infused crème brûlée filling, nestled in a bed of matcha and black sesame seeds, atop a buttery pastry crust, finished with a caramelized sugar crust.
tips: Make sure to not overmix the pastry dough, as it can become tough.
Use high-quality matcha powder for the best flavor and color.
Let the tarts cool completely before serving, as they will be easier to handle.
variations: Replace the heavy cream with a non-dairy milk alternative, such as almond or soy milk, for a dairy-free version.
Add a pinch of salt to the cream mixture to balance the sweetness.

steps.instruction: Preheat the oven to 180°C. Roll out the pastry dough to a thickness of 3mm and cut into 6 equal squares.

In a medium saucepan, whisk together the heavy cream, granulated sugar, and saffron threads. Cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the sugar has dissolved and the mixture is hot but not boiling.
In a small bowl, whisk together the egg yolks. Gradually add the warm cream mixture to the egg yolks, whisking constantly.
Pour the mixture into the pre-baked pastry tart shells and smooth the tops. Sprinkle with matcha powder and black sesame seeds.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the edges are set and the centers are still slightly jiggly. Remove from the oven and let cool to room temperature.
Just before serving, sprinkle a thin layer of granulated sugar over the top of each tart. Caramelize the sugar with a kitchen torch or under the broiler.

meals.name: Churro Waffle Sandwiches with Spiced Apple Cider Glaze and Coconut Whipped Cream
meals.description: Crispy churro-inspired waffles filled with a sweet and spicy apple cider glaze, topped with a dollop of coconut whipped cream and a sprinkle of cinnamon sugar.
meals.sourceInspiration: Latin American and American dessert traditions
tips: Make sure to not overmix the batter, as it can become tough.
Use high-quality apple cider for the best flavor.
Let the waffles cool completely before assembling the sandwiches, as they will be easier to handle.
variations: Replace the apple cider with a different type of milk or cream, such as almond or soy milk, for a non-dairy version.
Add a sprinkle of cinnamon or nutmeg to the coconut whipped cream for extra flavor.

steps.instruction: Preheat the waffle iron according to the manufacturer's instructions. In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, granulated sugar, and cinnamon.
In a large bowl, whisk together the melted butter, eggs, and apple cider. Add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients and stir until just combined. The batter should still be slightly lumpy.
Pour approximately 1/4 cup of the batter onto the center of the waffle iron and spread it out to the edges. Cook for 3-5 minutes, or until the waffles are crispy and golden brown.

To make the spiced apple cider glaze, combine the apple cider, granulated sugar, cinnamon, and nutmeg in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil over medium heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5-7 minutes, or until the glaze has thickened slightly.
To make the coconut whipped cream, chill the coconut cream in the refrigerator overnight. Scoop out the solid coconut cream and whip it with a stand mixer or hand mixer until light and fluffy.
Assemble the churro waffle sandwiches by placing a dollop of coconut whipped cream on top of a waffle, followed by a drizzle of spiced apple cider glaze and a sprinkle of cinnamon sugar.

meals.name: Black Sesame and Matcha Tiramisu with Coconut Cream and Sesame Brittle
meals.description: A creamy and decadent tiramisu dessert infused with the flavors of black sesame and matcha, topped with a dollop of coconut cream and a sprinkle of sesame brittle.
tips: Make sure to not overmix the butter and sugar mixture, as it can become too stiff.
Use high-quality matcha powder for the best flavor and color.
Let the tiramisu chill in the refrigerator for at least 3 hours before serving, as it will be easier to slice and serve.
variations: Replace the ladyfingers with a different type of cookie or cake, such as savoiardi or pound cake, for a different texture.
Add a sprinkle of cinnamon or nutmeg to the coconut cream for extra flavor.

steps.instruction: In a medium bowl, whisk together the granulated sugar, black sesame seeds, and matcha powder.
In a large bowl, whisk together the softened butter and egg yolks. Add the sugar mixture to the butter mixture and whisk until smooth.
Dip each ladyfinger into the coffee for about 3-5 seconds on each side. They should be soft and pliable but not too wet.

To assemble the tiramisu, start with a layer of ladyfingers in the bottom of a serving dish. Spread half of the butter and sugar mixture over the ladyfingers. Repeat the layers, ending with a layer of the butter and sugar mixture on top.
To make the coconut cream, chill the coconut cream in the refrigerator overnight. Scoop out the solid coconut cream and whip it with a stand mixer or hand mixer until light and fluffy.
To make the sesame brittle, heat the sesame oil in a small saucepan over medium heat. Add the black sesame seeds and cook, stirring constantly, until the seeds are fragrant and lightly toasted.

meals.name: Stuffed Grape Leaves with Herbed Rice
meals.description: Tender grape leaves filled with a flavorful mixture of herbed rice, served with a side of tangy lemon sauce.
tips: Use short-grain rice for the best results.
Don't overfill the grape leaves, as they will expand during cooking.
variations: Add some heat with a pinch of cayenne pepper.
Use quinoa instead of rice for a gluten-free option.

steps.instruction: Prepare the grape leaves by removing the stems and blanching them in boiling water for 30 seconds.
Mix the herbed rice ingredients together, including the cooked rice, parsley, dill, lemon juice, and olive oil.
Assemble the stuffed grape leaves by placing a tablespoon of the herbed rice mixture onto the center of each grape leaf and rolling them up.
Cook the stuffed grape leaves in boiling water for 20-25 minutes, or until the rice is tender.

meals.name: Roasted Cauliflower with Tahini Sauce and Pine Nuts
meals.description: Crispy roasted cauliflower florets smothered in a creamy tahini sauce and topped with toasted pine nuts.
tips: Don't overcrowd the baking sheet, as this can prevent the cauliflower from roasting evenly.
Adjust the amount of garlic to your taste.
variations: Add some heat with a pinch of cayenne pepper.
Use almonds instead of pine nuts for a different flavor.

steps.instruction: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Toss the cauliflower florets with olive oil, salt, and pepper, and roast in the oven for 20-25 minutes, or until crispy.
Prepare the tahini sauce by blending tahini, lemon juice, garlic, and water together until smooth.
Toast the pine nuts in a dry pan over medium heat for 2-3 minutes, or until fragrant.

meals.name: Saffron-Infused Freekeh with Caramelized Onions and Sumac
meals.description: Nutty freekeh grains infused with saffron and mixed with caramelized onions, sumac, and a hint of cinnamon.
tips: Use high-quality saffron threads for the best flavor.
Don't overcook the freekeh grains, as they can become mushy.
variations: Add some heat with a pinch of cayenne pepper.
Use bulgur instead of freekeh for a different texture.

steps.instruction: Rinse the freekeh grains and soak them in water for 30 minutes.
Caramelize the onions in olive oil over medium heat for 20-25 minutes, or until dark golden brown.
Infuse the freekeh grains with saffron by soaking them in hot water with saffron threads for 10-15 minutes.
Mix the cooked freekeh grains with caramelized onions, sumac, cinnamon, and a pinch of salt.

meals.name: Shakshuka with Merguez and Feta
meals.description: A rich and flavorful North African-inspired breakfast dish featuring spicy merguez sausage, creamy feta cheese, and runny eggs in a vibrant bell pepper sauce.
meals.sourceInspiration: North African and Middle Eastern cuisine
tips: Use high-quality merguez sausage for the best flavor.
Don't overcrowd the skillet, cook the sausage and vegetables in batches if necessary.
variations: Replace the merguez sausage with chorizo or other spicy sausage.
Add some chopped fresh parsley or cilantro for extra freshness.

steps.instruction: Heat the olive oil in a large cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced merguez sausage and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the diced bell peppers, chopped onions, and minced garlic, and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 10 minutes.
Make 4 wells in the vegetable mixture and crack an egg into each well.
Transfer the skillet to the oven and bake at 200°C for 15-20 minutes, or until the eggs are cooked to your desired doneness.

meals.name: Greek Stuffed Portobello Mushrooms
meals.description: A hearty and flavorful breakfast dish featuring meaty portobello mushrooms stuffed with a tangy feta and spinach mixture, perfect for a special occasion.
tips: Use high-quality feta cheese for the best flavor.
Don't overfill the mushrooms, leave a little room for the filling to expand during baking.
variations: Add some chopped sun-dried tomatoes or Kalamata olives to the feta and spinach mixture for extra flavor.
Replace the portobello mushrooms with other varieties, such as cremini or shiitake.

steps.instruction: Preheat the oven to 200°C.
Clean and prepare the portobello mushrooms by removing the stems and scraping out the gills.
In a bowl, mix together the crumbled feta cheese, chopped spinach, minced garlic, and lemon zest.
Divide the feta and spinach mixture among the mushroom caps, spooning it into the cavity of each mushroom.
Drizzle the tops with olive oil and season with salt and black pepper.
Bake the stuffed mushrooms in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until they're tender and the filling is heated through.

meals.name: Turkish-Style Menemen with Sucuk and Eggs
meals.description: A spicy and savory Turkish-inspired breakfast dish featuring crispy sucuk sausage, creamy eggs, and a blend of aromatic spices, perfect for a hearty morning meal.
tips: Use high-quality sucuk sausage for the best flavor.
Don't overcook the eggs, scramble them until they're just set.
variations: Add some chopped fresh parsley or dill for extra freshness.
Replace the sucuk sausage with other spicy sausages, such as chorizo or merguez.

steps.instruction: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced sucuk sausage and cook until crispy, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped onions, minced garlic, diced bell peppers, and diced tomatoes, and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 10 minutes.
Push the vegetables to one side of the skillet, then crack in the eggs and scramble them until cooked through.
Mix the eggs with the vegetables and sucuk sausage, and season with paprika, cumin, salt, and black pepper.

meals.name: Korean-Style BBQ Beef Tacos
meals.description: A fusion of Korean and Mexican flavors, featuring marinated beef short ribs, kimchi slaw, and cilantro in a crispy taco shell.
tips: Use a meat thermometer to ensure the beef is cooked to a safe internal temperature.
Let the beef rest for a few minutes before slicing to allow the juices to redistribute.
variations: For a vegetarian version, substitute the beef with marinated portobello mushrooms or tofu.
For a spicy kick, add diced jalapenos or serrano peppers to the kimchi slaw.

steps.instruction: In a large bowl, whisk together Gochujang, soy sauce, brown sugar, garlic, and ginger to make the marinade.
Add the beef short ribs to the marinade and mix well to coat. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat. Remove the beef from the marinade and cook for 5-7 minutes per side, or until cooked to desired level of doneness.
Warm the taco shells according to package instructions.
Assemble the tacos by slicing the cooked beef into thin strips and placing it into the taco shells. Top with kimchi slaw, cilantro, and any desired additional toppings.

meals.name: Japanese-Style Teriyaki Chicken Bento Box
meals.description: A traditional Japanese-inspired bento box featuring teriyaki chicken, steamed rice, and stir-fried vegetables, all carefully arranged to create a visually appealing meal.
tips: Use short-grain Japanese rice for the best results.
Arrange the ingredients in the bento box to create a visually appealing meal.
variations: For a vegetarian version, substitute the chicken with marinated tofu or tempeh.
For a gluten-free version, use gluten-free soy sauce and teriyaki sauce.

steps.instruction: In a small saucepan, combine teriyaki sauce, soy sauce, sake, mirin, and brown sugar. Bring to a boil over medium heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
Add the chicken to the teriyaki sauce and cook until cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per side.
Cook the Japanese rice according to package instructions.
In a large skillet or wok, heat 1 tablespoon of oil over medium-high heat. Add the carrots and green beans and stir-fry until tender-crisp, about 3-5 minutes.
Assemble the bento boxes by placing a portion of steamed rice, teriyaki chicken, and stir-fried vegetables into each box.

meals.name: Thai-Style Green Curry with Shrimp and Vegetables
meals.description: A spicy and flavorful Thai-inspired green curry featuring succulent shrimp, crispy vegetables, and a rich coconut milk-based sauce, served with steamed rice.
tips: Use high-quality green curry paste for the best flavor.
Adjust the level of spiciness to your liking by adding more or less green curry paste.
variations: For a vegetarian version, substitute the shrimp with marinated tofu or tempeh.
For a dairy-free version, use coconut milk instead of traditional milk or cream.

steps.instruction: In a large skillet or wok, heat 1 tablespoon of oil over medium heat. Add the green curry paste and cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes.
Add the coconut milk, fish sauce, lime juice, and brown sugar to the skillet. Bring to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Add the shrimp and bell peppers to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until the shrimp are pink and the vegetables are tender-crisp, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in the chopped Thai basil and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Serve the green curry over steamed Thai rice.

meals.name: Bangladeshi Beef Rezala
meals.description: A rich and flavorful Bangladeshi dish made with beef, onions, garlic, ginger, and a variety of spices, all slow-cooked in a creamy sauce.
tips: Use a variety of spices to add depth and complexity to the dish.
Adjust the amount of chili powder to your desired level of spiciness.
variations: For a vegetarian version, substitute the beef with extra-firm tofu or tempeh.
To make the dish more substantial, serve with a side of steamed vegetables or a salad.

steps.instruction: Heat 15ml of oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add the cumin powder, coriander powder, turmeric powder, and red chili powder, and let them sizzle for a few seconds.
Add the onion, garlic, and ginger to the pan, and cook until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the beef to the pan, and cook until it is browned and cooked through, about 10-12 minutes.
Add the yogurt and heavy cream to the pan, and stir to combine. Bring the mixture to a simmer, and cook until the sauce has thickened, about 10-15 minutes.
Season the dish with salt to taste, and garnish with chopped cilantro if desired.

meals.name: Kueh Pie Tee
meals.description: Crispy pastry cups filled with a sweet and spicy mixture of shredded vegetables and sometimes meat or seafood, typically served as a snack at Southeast Asian gatherings.
meals.sourceInspiration: Peranakan cuisine, a blend of Chinese, Malay, and Indian culinary traditions found in Singapore and Malaysia.
tips: Use a variety of colorful vegetables to make the filling more visually appealing.
Adjust the amount of sambal chili paste to suit your desired level of spiciness.
variations: Add cooked and shredded chicken or shrimp to the filling for added protein.
Use different types of pastry dough, such as wonton wrappers or spring roll wrappers, for a varying texture.

steps.instruction: Combine flour and salt in a mixing bowl, then gradually add ice-cold water to form a dough.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and pliable.
Divide the dough into small balls and roll each into a thin sheet.
Heat vegetable oil in a frying pan and fry the pastry cups until crispy and golden brown.
Fill the pastry cups with the shredded vegetable mixture and serve immediately.

meals.name: Martabak Telur
meals.description: A stuffed pancake filled with a mixture of eggs, vegetables, and sometimes meat, typically served as a snack or light meal in Southeast Asian countries.
meals.sourceInspiration: Indonesian and Malaysian cuisine, where martabak is a popular street food snack.
tips: Use a variety of colorful vegetables to make the filling more visually appealing.
Adjust the amount of eggs to suit your desired level of richness.
variations: Add cooked and shredded chicken or beef to the filling for added protein.
Use different types of flour, such as whole wheat or rice flour, for a varying texture.

steps.instruction: Combine flour, eggs, and a pinch of salt in a mixing bowl to form a batter.
Heat a non-stick pan with vegetable oil and pour in the batter to form a thin pancake.
Add the shredded vegetable mixture to one half of the pancake and fold the other half over to enclose the filling.
Cook the martabak for another 2-3 minutes until the bottom is golden brown, then flip and cook the other side.

meals.name: Tres Leches Cake
meals.description: A moist and decadent sponge cake soaked in three types of milk, resulting in a sweet and creamy dessert. This traditional Latin American dessert is a crowd-pleaser.
tips: Make sure to not overmix the batter, as this can result in a dense cake.
Let the cake cool completely before serving, as this will help the milk to absorb evenly.
variations: For a gluten-free version, substitute the all-purpose flour with a gluten-free flour blend.
For a dairy-free version, substitute the whole milk, evaporated milk, and heavy cream with non-dairy milk alternatives.

steps.instruction: Preheat the oven to 180°C and grease a 20cm round cake pan.
In a medium bowl, whisk together flour and sugar.
In a large bowl, whisk together eggs, whole milk, and vanilla extract.
Add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients and mix until just combined.
Pour the batter into the prepared cake pan and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove the cake from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes.
Poke holes all over the top of the cake with a skewer.
Pour the evaporated milk, heavy cream, and whole milk evenly over the top of the cake.

meals.name: Flan de Coco
meals.description: A creamy and rich coconut flan, topped with a layer of caramel sauce and toasted coconut flakes. This dessert is a tropical twist on the traditional Latin American flan.
tips: Make sure to not overbake the flans, as this can cause them to become too firm.
Let the flans cool completely before serving, as this will help them to set properly.
variations: For a dairy-free version, substitute the sweetened condensed milk with a non-dairy milk alternative.
For a vegan version, substitute the eggs with a flax egg and the sweetened condensed milk with a non-dairy milk alternative.

steps.instruction: Preheat the oven to 180°C.
In a medium bowl, whisk together sweetened condensed milk, coconut milk, and vanilla extract.
In a large bowl, whisk together eggs and granulated sugar.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and mix until well combined.
Pour the mixture into 6 ramekins or small cups.
Place the ramekins in a large baking dish and add hot water to come halfway up the sides.
Bake for 45 minutes, or until the edges are set and the centers are still slightly jiggly.
Remove the flans from the water bath and let them cool to room temperature.
Top with caramel sauce and toasted coconut flakes.

meals.name: Saffron Infused Mexican Street Corn
meals.description: Delicious and aromatic street corn infused with saffron and a hint of lime, perfect for accompanying your favorite Mexican dishes.
tips: Use high-quality saffron threads for the best flavor and aroma.
Don't over-steep the saffron, as it can become bitter.
variations: For a vegan version, replace mayonnaise with a vegan alternative and omit the cotija cheese.
Add some heat to your street corn by sprinkling a pinch of cayenne pepper on top.

steps.instruction: Preheat grill to medium-high heat.
Grill corn on the cob for 10-12 minutes, turning frequently.
Mix saffron threads with lime juice and let it steep for 5 minutes.
Slather mayonnaise on grilled corn, then sprinkle with cotija cheese, chili powder, and saffron-lime mixture.

meals.name: Roasted Poblano and Sweet Potato Gratin
meals.description: A rich and creamy gratin featuring roasted poblanos and sweet potatoes, perfect as a side dish for your favorite Mexican meals.
tips: Use high-quality poblanos for the best flavor.
Don't over-roast the sweet potatoes, as they can become too soft.
variations: For a vegan version, replace heavy cream with a non-dairy alternative and omit the queso fresco.
Add some heat to your gratin by sprinkling a pinch of diced jalapeño on top.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
Roast poblanos in the oven for 30-40 minutes, or until charred and blistered.
Peel and dice the roasted poblanos.
Thinly slice the sweet potatoes and layer them in a baking dish with the roasted poblanos, onion, and garlic.
Pour heavy cream over the top and sprinkle with queso fresco.
Bake in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until golden and bubbly.

meals.name: Cilantro Lime Rice with Toasted Pepitas
meals.description: Fresh and flavorful Mexican-style rice dish featuring toasted pepitas and a burst of lime juice, perfect as a side for your favorite Mexican meals.
tips: Use fresh cilantro for the best flavor.
Don't over-toast the pepitas, as they can become too brown.
variations: For a vegan version, replace lime juice with a non-dairy alternative.
Add some heat to your rice by sprinkling a pinch of diced jalapeño on top.

steps.instruction: Heat olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add pepitas and toast for 2-3 minutes, or until fragrant and lightly browned.
Add uncooked rice to the saucepan and stir to combine with the toasted pepitas.
Add water to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the water is absorbed and the rice is tender.
Stir in chopped cilantro, lime juice, and salt.

meals.name: Italian Breakfast Strata
meals.description: A layered Italian breakfast dish made with bread, eggs, and cheese, perfect for a special occasion. This strata is a hearty and satisfying breakfast that can be prepared ahead of time.
meals.sourceInspiration: Italian cuisine, specifically the Tuscan region
tips: Use a day-old bread to make the strata, as it will absorb the liquid better.
Let the strata rest for 10-15 minutes before serving to allow the eggs to set.
variations: Add some diced ham or bacon to the strata for a non-vegetarian version.
Use different types of cheese, such as parmesan or mozzarella, for a unique flavor.

steps.instruction: Preheat the oven to 180°C.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, salt, and pepper.
Add the cubed bread, shredded cheese, and chopped spinach to the bowl and stir until the bread is well coated.
Pour the mixture into a greased 20x20 cm baking dish and bake for 35-40 minutes, or until the strata is puffed and golden brown.

meals.name: Cannoli French Toast
meals.description: A creative Italian breakfast dish that combines the crispy shell of a cannoli with the sweetness of French toast. This dish is perfect for a special occasion or a weekend brunch.
meals.sourceInspiration: Sicilian cuisine, specifically the city of Palermo
tips: Use high-quality cannoli shells to ensure they stay crispy.
Don't overfill the cannoli shells, as the ricotta cheese will spread when cooked.
variations: Add some chopped nuts or cinnamon to the ricotta cheese mixture for extra flavor.
Use different types of cheese, such as mascarpone or cream cheese, for a unique flavor.

steps.instruction: In a shallow dish, whisk together the eggs and milk.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Dip each bread slice into the egg mixture, coating both sides evenly.
Place the bread slices in the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown.
Fill the cannoli shells with the ricotta cheese mixture and place on top of the French toast.

meals.name: Italian Breakfast Frittata
meals.description: A classic Italian breakfast dish made with eggs, vegetables, and cheese, perfect for a weekend brunch. This frittata is a versatile and delicious meal that can be customized with your favorite ingredients.
meals.sourceInspiration: Italian cuisine, specifically the region of Tuscany
tips: Use a variety of colorful vegetables to make the frittata visually appealing.
Don't overmix the egg mixture, as it can become tough and rubbery.
variations: Add some diced ham or bacon to the frittata for a non-vegetarian version.
Use different types of cheese, such as parmesan or mozzarella, for a unique flavor.

steps.instruction: Preheat the oven to 180°C.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, salt, and pepper.
Add the shredded cheese, sliced zucchini, sliced bell peppers, and chopped onions to the bowl and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a greased 20x20 cm baking dish and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the frittata is set and golden brown.

meals.name: Tarte Flambée
